name: Ascent to Peñalmonte — circular from Arnedillo
name_original: Subida a Peñalmonte — circular desde Arnedillo
region: Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa
region_intro: Hiking trails in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa traverse the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Rioja Baja. The region features prominent limestone walls, the scenic Cidacos River Valley, and pine forests at higher elevations. Peaks like Peña Isasa offer panoramic views, while the area also includes natural thermal springs and former railway lines converted into greenways. Best hiking trails in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa The most popular hiking route is Peña del Castillo and Cidacos Greenway Loop, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 1 minute to complete. This moderate route follows parts of the Cidacos Greenway and offers views of Peña del Castillo. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ascent to Peñalmonte — circular from Arnedillo, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.1 km) path. This trail features a sustained ascent to Peñalmonte, starting and ending in Arnedillo. Local hikers also love the Peña Isasa Loop from Palomares, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain towards the mountain, often completed in about 3 hours 39 minutes. Hiking in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa is defined by limestone walls, river valleys, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy greenways to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails in the region, offering a variety of experiences from easy walks to more challenging mountain ascents. The komoot community has explored more than 20 routes here.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hikes in this region?
A: The terrain is quite diverse, featuring prominent limestone walls, scenic river valleys like the Cidacos, and pine forests at higher elevations. You'll find paths ranging from the relatively flat &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199657133/pena-del-castillo-and-cidacos-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt; to the more rugged ascents towards peaks like Peñalmonte and Peña Isasa.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hikes?
A: Yes, the region offers 8 easy routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, for example, provides a pleasant and relatively flat option, often following old railway lines.

Q: What are the best viewpoints in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa?
A: For spectacular panoramic views, consider hiking to the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/a&gt;. From there, you can see the Cidacos and Linares valleys, as well as towns like Arnedo and Calahorra. The region also features a Vulture Viewpoint (Mirador del Buitre) near Arnedillo, where you can observe griffon vultures.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, several popular routes are circular. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1200857406/ascent-to-penalmonte-circular-from-arnedillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Peñalmonte — circular from Arnedillo&lt;/a&gt; offers a sustained climb with a return to the starting point. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1242057510/pena-isasa-loop-from-palomares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa Loop from Palomares&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through varied terrain around the mountain.

Q: What unique natural features can I explore while hiking?
A: The region is known for its impressive limestone walls, especially at the entrance to Arnedillo. You can also experience the natural thermal springs of Arnedillo, known as 'Las Pozas,' where you can soak in warm waters after your hike. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt; also features old railway tunnels and scenic river views.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about this region?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from river valleys to mountain peaks, and the unique combination of active hiking with the relaxation of the thermal springs in Arnedillo.

Q: Can I combine hiking with other activities, like visiting historical sites?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the natural beauty, the region has historical elements. On &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/a&gt;, you can find megalithic monuments. The town of Arnedillo itself has a rich history, including a castle. You can also visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5344963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Préjano Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which is another nearby attraction.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or landmarks along the trails?
A: Yes, you can encounter several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight in itself, offering views of limestone mountains and lush river valleys. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1751534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedillo&lt;/a&gt; is famous for its thermal baths, and the summit of &lt;a href="https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pe%C3%B1a_Isasa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent landmark.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter while hiking?
A: The region is home to colonies of griffon vultures, which can often be observed soaring above the limestone cliffs, particularly near Arnedillo. The diverse landscapes also support various other bird species and local fauna.

Q: Is it possible to hike to Peña Isasa?
A: Yes, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/a&gt; is a popular hiking destination. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1200958076/pena-isasa-loop-from-prejano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa Loop from Préjano&lt;/a&gt; offer a challenging ascent through varied terrain, including pine forests, leading to its prominent summit.

Q: Can I visit the thermal baths in Arnedillo after a hike?
A: Absolutely. The natural thermal springs of Arnedillo, known as 'Las Pozas,' are a perfect way to relax and recover after a day of hiking. These rustic hot springs are located on the banks of the Cidacos River and are known for their warm, mineral-rich waters. There's even a specific route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedillo and thermal baths – Hot spring pools loop from Arnedillo&lt;/a&gt;, that incorporates them.

Small height close to the antennae.

The top of Peñalmonte has a mailbox, and several painted in yellow on the rock, indicating danger and in another area the cardinal points. There is also a nativity scene with various figurines. The views are magnificent, in front of the Serralay and the Cabimonteros. Following the ridge you can see Peña Isasa and Moncayo behind. It is worth going up on a clear day, but without much heat.
